execution_summary: Built a Python 3.10-slim Docker image with pandas, numpy, statsmodels, and scipy. Launched the container and executed the Python replication script on the SCORE_ALL_DATA.csv dataset. The initial run failed due to object dtype in the regression matrix; I revised the script to coerce numeric columns and strip strings. The rerun succeeded, estimating an OLS model of average desirability on clarity, experimental condition, and their interaction. The focal interaction was negative and statistically significant (p = 0.022), consistent with the preregistered hypothesis. Outputs (JSON and TXT summaries) were saved. No deviations from the preregistered analysis beyond necessary data-type cleaning.

results.hypothesis_tested: In a 2x2 between-subjects design, the interaction between self-concept clarity (clear vs conflicted) and rating distribution (bimodal vs unimodal) on average desirability is negative, indicating a crossover.

findings_summary.outcome_name: Main effect of experimental (bimodal vs unimodal)

tables.table_description: Coefficients, SEs, t, p for intercept, clarity, experimental, and their interaction; also includes R-squared and cell means.
Parsed results including coefficients, model fit, and cell means/counts.
